main.title: Why Are You Lazy?
main.description:
You had all day to do one thing. You didn't, and then came the guilt. Here's the twist: the guilt is the lie, and where it comes from is 1,600 years old and way weirder than you think.

What you'll learn:
- Why evolution built you to do as little as possible (a feature, not a flaw)
- The tiny accountant in your head approving everything you don't do
- The desert demon that became the deadly sin of sloth
- Who rebranded resting as a moral failure (and why)

Be honest: are you watching this instead of doing something? Tell us below.

SOURCES
- Brain/body energy conservation and the "law of least effort" (Big Think; Simon Fraser University walking-energetics; Cheval & Boisgontier).
- Acedia, the "noonday demon": Evagrius Ponticus's eight temptations (4th c.); Gregory the Great's seven deadly sins; Aquinas on sloth. (Atlas Obscura; standard references.)
- The Protestant work ethic and industrial-era moralizing of idleness (Max Weber, framed as a widely-cited thesis).

This video uses AI-assisted narration and illustrations.

#psychology #yourbrain #motivation #history #wonder
